reasoning:

Assuming a standard geometric Brownian motion model with a historical annualized volatility for MSFT of ~25% and an expected return (drift) of around 12% (optimistic to average tech premium), the probability of the stock closing above $421.50 is between 40% and 48%. Given the recent downward momentum (YTD down ~7%) and the broader tech sector pressures mentioned in recent news, an estimate slightly below neutral is appropriate.
